name: Discovery endpoint
outcome: A relying party can fetch .well-known/openid-configuration and learn the server's OIDC capabilities and endpoint URLs. The document is spec-conformant: id_token_signing_alg_values_supported is ["RS256"] (satisfies OIDC Discovery §3 RS256-mandatory) and all four REQUIRED endpoint URLs are present.
scope: Add the discovery document builder and the AuthorizationServer.openidConfiguration() method. ES256 is not advertised in v1 — see PRD out_of_scope. Adds a docs page for discovery as part of this issue.
acceptance_criteria: openidConfiguration() returns a well-formed document with: issuer (from options), all four REQUIRED endpoint URLs (authorization_endpoint, token_endpoint, userinfo_endpoint, jwks_uri — a missing userinfo_endpoint makes UserInfo undiscoverable), and auto-derived capability fields
id_token_signing_alg_values_supported is ["RS256"] (RFC-compliant single-value list — RS256 is the OIDC Discovery §3 mandatory algorithm; ES256 is deferred to v2)
code_challenge_methods_supported is ["S256"] (S256-only; the grant still accepts plain for backward compatibility but discovery does not advertise it)

scopes_supported lists the standard OIDC scopes the library recognizes — openid, profile, email, address, phone. offline_access is intentionally absent (consistent with issue 4 dropping it from auto-recognition); consumers add custom scopes via the metadata override

Consumer metadata override shallow-merges into the generated document EXCEPT for security-critical fields: issuer, jwks_uri, and id_token_signing_alg_values_supported are NOT overridable (a consumer override would silently weaken the security posture)
Document is served with Content-Type: application/json and Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600 (RPs cache discovery)
steps: src/oidc — add a discovery document builder that auto-derives response_types_supported, grant_types_supported, id_token_signing_alg_values_supported (["RS256"] only), subject_types_supported, and token_endpoint_auth_methods_supported

discovery builder — set code_challenge_methods_supported to ["S256"] only; the grant still accepts a plain code_challenge_method for backward compatibility but discovery does not advertise it (security BCP)

discovery builder — set scopes_supported to ["openid", "profile", "email", "address", "phone"] (no offline_access in v1); the library has no scope registry, so any non-standard scopes must be supplied by the consumer via the metadata override

discovery builder — include the consumer-supplied endpoint URLs from the oidc config block; assert all four REQUIRED URLs are present at construction (issue 2 guard); shallow-merge the optional metadata override but reject overrides of issuer, jwks_uri, and id_token_signing_alg_values_supported (throw at construction if the override sets these)
AuthorizationServer — add an openidConfiguration() method returning the document as a ResponseInterface with Content-Type and Cache-Control headers set

test/unit — auto-derived fields; the code-only and public-subject constraints; the S256-only code_challenge_methods; the four-REQUIRED-URLs assertion; the metadata merge; the security-critical-fields override rejection (each of issuer / jwks_uri / id_token_signing_alg_values_supported attempted separately)
docs site — add a discovery endpoint reference page as part of this issue, not deferred
notes: The library does not own routing, so endpoint URLs are supplied explicitly by the consumer. issuer is reused from top-level options. scopes_supported lists only the standard OIDC scopes (no offline_access in v1 — consistent with issue 4); consumer-specific scopes come via metadata override. claims_supported is unknowable by the library and only appears if the consumer provides it via override. id_token_signing_alg_values_supported is ["RS256"] only because v1 is RSA-only — see PRD decisions for the ES256 deferral rationale (single-key model cannot satisfy OIDC Discovery §3 RS256-mandatory with an ES256 key). Security-critical metadata override rejection (issuer, jwks_uri, id_token_signing_alg_values_supported) prevents a consumer from silently weakening discovery.
